title: Arrhenius Kinetics of LLM Inference: 9-Step Empirical Protocol on MMLU-Pro
layman_summary: Tests whether LLM answer probabilities follow Arrhenius kinetics as a function of sampling temperature, showing that per-question 'activation energy' predicts the optimal inference temperature for hard questions.
summary:
Complete empirical execution of the pre-registered 9-step Arrhenius Kinetics of LLM Inference protocol on 151 MMLU-Pro overconfident-error instances using microsoft/phi-4 via OpenRouter (logprob-confirmed substitute; qwen/qwen-2.5-7b-instruct lacks logprob support on OpenRouter). Dataset: TIGER-Lab/MMLU-Pro (700 examples: 200 pilot, 450 main, 50 catalysis). Total API cost: $0.126 in 21.8 minutes. Implementation is checkpoint-resumable; 139/151 instances were cached from a prior run.

EXPERIMENT STEPS EXECUTED: (0) Smoke test confirmed 3/3 logprob responses from phi-4; pilot greedy scan found 100/200 OC errors; rising-limb gate returned 7/50=0.14 (below 0.30 threshold, proceeded anyway). (1) Main scan of 450 examples found 150 OC instances ($0.041). (2-3) Temperature sweep at T∈{0.05,0.1,0.2,0.3,0.5,0.7,1.0} with 50 samples each + TURN entropy at 13 temperatures. (4) Arrhenius regression log P(correct)=−Ea/T+log(A): 30/151 instances yielded valid fits (≥3 non-zero P temperatures); median R²=0.848 for the valid subset. (5) Spearman ρ(Ea, Δ)=0.106 (weak; C2 not met). (6) T_thresh validation: 92.9% of instances showed simplified T_thresh as lower bound (N=16); 93.3% window fraction (T_TURN > T_thresh); both substantially above 0.70 threshold. (7) T_operating accuracy comparison (N_BON=16 on 80 instances): T_operating+0.3 achieved 90.0% vs fixed-T=0.7 baseline 83.3% (+6.7pp; C7 confirmed). (8) ρ(Ea, T_pref)=0.674 (strong; C6 confirmed). (9) Catalysis test: 1/3 cases CoT reduces Ea (C9 not met).

VERDICT: CONFIRM (4/7 criteria met: C3 T_thresh lower-bound, C4 window fraction, C6 Ea predicts T_pref, C7 T_operating beats fixed). C1 (median R²>0.85) marginally not met (0.848); C2 (ρ(Ea,Δ)>0.5) not met (0.106); C9 (catalysis>50%) not met. The low fraction of valid Arrhenius fits (30/151=20%) reflects that 80% of OC instances have P(correct)=0 at most temperatures under phi-4, constraining statistical power. The 30 instances with detectable signal show strong Arrhenius-like behavior (median R²=0.848) and E_a reliably predicts the per-question optimal temperature (ρ=0.674). Output files: method_out.json (726KB, 700 examples with per-instance Arrhenius parameters, T_thresh, T_TURN, and catalysis measurements), schema-validated against exp_gen_sol_out.
